title: Boolean literals should be avoided in comparisons
description: Boolean literals should not be used in comparisonsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellclumsyWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoA boolean literal can be represented in two different ways:trueorfalse. They can be combined with logical operators (!, &&, ||, ==, !=) to produce logical expressions that represent truth values. However, comparing a boolean literal to a variable or expression that evaluates to a boolean value is unnecessary and can make the code harder to read and understand.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Avoid comparing with boolean literals - use the boolean value directly.
